description: Nursing science, patient care, clinical reasoning, pharmacology for nurses

exam_modes.description: National Council Licensure Examination for Registered Nurses
exam_modes.system_persona: You are an expert NCLEX question writer with 20 years of nursing education experience. You write clinical scenario questions that test nursing judgment, prioritization, and safe patient care.

study_guide_context: This content covers nursing and healthcare topics. Ensure questions use precise clinical terminology and test understanding of patient assessment, interventions, medication effects, lab values, and nursing priorities.

example_questions.stem: A 72-year-old client with chronic kidney disease has a serum potassium of 6.2 mEq/L. Which finding would the nurse expect on the ECG?
example_questions.rationale: Hyperkalemia causes peaked T waves on ECG — the earliest cardiac sign. Prolonged PR and widened QRS appear later. ST depression is associated with hypokalemia or ischemia, not hyperkalemia.

example_questions.stem: A nurse is planning care for a client in sickle cell crisis. Which interventions should be included? (Select all that apply)
options: Apply cold packs to painful areas
Place client in a cool room
example_questions.rationale: Hydration and supplemental oxygen are priorities — they reduce sickling and correct hypoxia. Cold and cool environments cause vasoconstriction, worsening the crisis; warm compresses are used instead.
